analysis_result: 36 unmatched tumor samples underwent WES without paired normal controls, based on the Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode field in the WES MAF (MOESM5) having the literal value 'unmatched'. This is one more than the 35 stated in the paper's Methods for Mutect2; the small discrepancy may reflect one sample included in the MAF but described differently in the text.
denominator_used: N = 52 unique Tumor_Sample_Barcode values in MOESM5 WES MAF; of these, 36 have Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode == 'unmatched' (tumor-only) and 16 have a matched normal control.
assumptions_made: Unmatched status inferred from Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode == 'unmatched' in the MAF, since there is no explicit caller column; a tumor is classified unmatched if all of its mutation rows have that value (none had both matched and unmatched rows); Tumor_Sample_Barcode in MOESM5 equals 'Tumor_' prefix + 'WES label' from MOESM4 for cross-reference.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - MOESM5 (WES MAF): 7457 rows, 123 columns. Key columns: Tumor_Sample_Barcode, Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode.
   - MOESM4 (Clinical Data): 108 samples, columns include Tumor_ID, WES, WES label.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - All MAF rows (n=7457 mutations).
   - Distinct Tumor_Sample_Barcode values: 52 WES samples total.

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode value distribution:
       'unmatched'                                   7298 rows
       Matched normal IDs (e.g., MPNST_0011_Pb_R_EX_Control_2215)  159 rows across 16 distinct normals
   - A tumor sample is considered 'unmatched' (tumor-only, no paired normal)
     if ALL of its MAF rows have Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode == 'unmatched'.

D. COMPUTATION
   - Unique Tumor_Sample_Barcode values where Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode == 'unmatched': 36.
   - Unique Tumor_Sample_Barcode values with a non-'unmatched' value (i.e., matched normal): 16.
   - Verified 0 tumor samples appear in both 'unmatched' and 'matched' rows (no overlap).
   - 34 of the 36 unmatched samples cross-reference to Clinical Data 'WES label' (when stripping 'Tumor_' prefix).

E. RESULT
   - 36 unmatched tumor samples underwent WES without paired normal controls (per MAF field).
   - Paper Methods state Mutect2 was used for 35 unmatched tumor samples; the data file shows 36.

F. CAVEATS
   - The MAF-based count (36) differs by 1 from the paper's stated 35. Possible explanation: one
     additional tumor-only sample may have been added to the final MAF, or Methods text rounded.
   - Two unmatched samples (M2242, RT3379) could not be cross-referenced in Clinical Data WES label.
   - No explicit caller column in the MAF to verify Mutect2 directly, but 'unmatched' semantics
     unambiguously identify tumor-only (no paired normal) samples.
